ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-27-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall specially appearing for Sheila Robello on behalf of Betty Wong (administrator).

At the first calling of the matter the court noted that Mr. Dawkins was not present. The matter was passed for Mrs. Hall to try and contact him. The matter was recalled at 10:50 a.m. Mrs. Hall stated that she had no luck reaching Mr. Dawkins, that the delays are significantly impacting the other beneficiaries, and that Mrs. Robello is asking the court to grant final distribution and that Sarah Lum's share be held as a reserve until her probate is open. Mrs. Hall also stated that they are concerned that if they wait any longer, they may have to open yet another probate. An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney William Dawkins to appear on September 24, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear. An order to show cause re sanctions is also being issued for the same date for Mr. Dawkins to show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against him for failure to appear. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission. Status on filing of the receipts on distribution is set for September 24, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-28-2024 Continued To Jun-25-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jubilee Menzies appeared by zoom for Betty Wong (administrator).

The court stated that this is a long living matter, that it did not have the opportunity to review exhibit A & B of the trust that is in the supplement that was recently filed, raised the issue of the request that Sara Lum's distribution go to her trust directly, and stated that it appears the funds should be placed in an estate for Sara Lum which would require opening probate. Mrs. Menzies stated that Sara's beneficiaries are represented by counsel, that this is their request, and spoke of the residuary clause in the trust and the pour over will. The court stated that absent any specific authority to distribute to the trust, the court will indicate that probate will need to be open. Mrs. Menzies stated that the authority for the alternate request to distribute the funds to the County Treasurer is under Probate Code 11850(d). The court stated that it is not convinced that it is appropriate to do so because it can be handled through probate. Mrs. Menzies stated that the beneficiaries and their attorney have repeatedly refused to open probate, that her client is elderly, that this is making things more difficult for her client and asked the court to allow distribution to the County Treasurer. An order to show cause is issued to Attorney William L. Dawkins to appear on June 25, 2024 and to provide points and authorities as to why he feels it is inappropriate to open probate. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is also continued to June 25, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mrs. Menzies stated that her client did not take any fees during special administration.

Judge: Roger T. Picquet. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

The court asked Mr. Dawkins what his clients' plans are to receive final distribution of their 18% share of the estate. Mr. Dawkins stated that he can't answer the court's question, but his clients do want to claim their interest through the grandmothers' will and the revocable trust, that they were named beneficiaries, and that it was Sarah Ann Lum's expressed intent. The court noted that no personal representative has been appointed to probate Sarah Lum's percentage. Mr. Dawkins stated that the great granddaughters have received control 16 different investment accounts and the real property in San Francisco, and that the Steiner Street property was sold. The court noted that Zeppelin's share will go to his trust, or to his wife individually. Mrs. Menzies stated that Zeppelin's intent was to create a trust and have a pour over will that corresponds with that trust, that both documents identify this portion of the estate and both specify that it goes to the wife, that his intent was that the will would pour over into the trust, and requested that if the court is requiring that probate be opened, to please make it clear. Mr. Dawkins stated that Sarah's will and her trust expressly wanted all of her estate to go to the great granddaughters, and that it has been done. Status on Appointment of Personal Representative of Deceased Executor, etc. is continued to June 27, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Oct-06-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jubilee Menzies, via Zoom, for Betty A. Wong, Personal Representative.

The court and Ms. Menzies discuss the status of this matter. The court queries counsel as to the status of an accounting. Ms. Menzies reports that there are some assets that need to be assessed and that there are pending subpoenas for records to confirm those assets. The court confirms that Ms. Menzies' client is the Administrator of this estate and should file a two-year interim accounting which will set distributions. Ms. Menzies advises that she believed that an accounting was not required as there are waivers from all beneficiaries. The court and counsel discuss the current Final Inventory and Appraisal (I&A) filed with the court and the court confirms that an Amended Final I&A will need to be filed.

After additional discussion, the Status Petition is taken under submission. An Order to Show Cause RE filing an Amended Final I&A and filing of waivers of Accounting is set for Jan-11-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. William Barry. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Saephan. Not Reported.

***After hearing (Oct-05-2022): Upon further consideration, the court will issue an Order to Show Cause RE the Filing of Deceased Executor Zeppelin Wong's final Account under probate code 10953(c) is set for Jan-11-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello for Betty Wong (Petitioner).

Ms. Robello stated that there are no grandchildren under twenty one years of age, that there possibly could be some greatgrandchildren, that there is no opposition, and that this is a companion petition with a spousal property petition and the Wife's probate. Ms. Robello also stated that the Executor who is deceased had been handling the property as the Husband's property, that she needs to get a determination on the spousal property petition in the Wife's estate to see what the assets will be and that her Client is coming in as a special administrator. The Court stated that they will need an administrator no matter what, that bond can be adjusted if needed and that it prefers not to keep stringing this along. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $1,260,000.00 is required. Status on close of estate is set for July 22, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
